items.text:
NOTICE: Extended Library Hours for Final Examinations

The Central Library will extend its operating hours during the final examination period, from December 8 through December 19. During this period, the building will remain open from 7:00 AM until 2:00 AM, Monday through Friday, and from 9:00 AM until midnight on weekends.

Several services will be available to support students:

- The second-floor computer lab will be staffed until 1:00 AM each night.
- Printing will be offered at a reduced rate of five cents per page, down from the usual ten cents.
- Group study rooms may be reserved online through the library portal for up to three hours.

Students are reminded that valid identification must be presented for entry after 11:00 PM. Quiet-study rules will be strictly enforced on the upper floors. Food is not permitted in the reading rooms, though covered drinks are allowed. Questions may be directed to the circulation desk or emailed to the Facilities Office.

questions.stem: According to the notice, when do the extended hours take effect?

options.A: From the eighth to the nineteenth of December
options.B: From the first to the twelfth of December
options.C: From the tenth to the twentieth of December
options.D: From the fifth to the fifteenth of December

questions.explanation: The notice states the extended hours run from December 8 through December 19.

questions.stem: According to the notice, how much will printing cost during the examination period?
questions.explanation: The notice says printing will be offered at a reduced rate of five cents per page.

questions.stem: What can be inferred about a student who arrives at 11:30 PM without identification?

options.A: That student will be denied entry
options.B: That student may enter the computer lab
options.C: That student can reserve a study room
options.D: That student will receive a printing discount

questions.explanation: The notice requires valid identification for entry after 11:00 PM, so a student without it at 11:30 PM would not be admitted.
